official recommended installation sources.

I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the

data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they want a reference guide. This is a knowledge/advice question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I'll provide a comprehensive answer directly.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS (iOS + Android):

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | clang, git, etc. |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android build toolchain |

## 2. Install .NET SDK

Download from **[dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)** — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that supports MAUI (currently .NET 8+).

Verify:
```bash
dotnet --version
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in Android SDK, Android emulator, iOS tooling, and OpenJDK automatically.

**Discover available versions:**
```bash
dotnet workload search maui
dotnet workload list          # shows what's installed
```

## 4. Accept Licenses & Configure Xcode

```bash
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
```

## 5. Android Setup (auto-provisioned)

The MAUI workload installs the Android SDK to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`. To manage manually or check versions:

```bash
dotnet android sdk list
dotnet android sdk install "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34"
```

For the emulator, create an AVD via Android Studio or:
```bash
dotnet android avd create --name Pixel_API_34 --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

## 6. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode simulator)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# Android (requires emulator running or device connected)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

## 7. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight editor with hot reload
- **Visual Studio for Mac successor**: Use VS Code with C# Dev Kit (VS for Mac is retired)
- **Android Studio** — only if you need advanced emulator/AVD management

## 8. Staying Current

```bash
dotnet workload update          # updates all workloads
dotnet sdk check                # shows available SDK updates
```

---

**Key official docs:**  
- [Microsoft .NET MAUI installation guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)  
- [.NET MAUI supported platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

The critical insight: `dotnet workload install maui` is the single command that provisions nearly everything (Android SDK, emulator images, JDK). Xcode is the only major prerequisite you must install separately.
